title: ChatStreamEnable::new takes Option<Vec<Id>> instead of Option<&[Id]>
description: ChatStreamEnable::new(data_types: Vec<ChatStreamDataType>, chat_ids: Option<Vec<Id>>, contact_ids: Option<Vec<Id>>) takes owned Vecs for the optional filter lists. All other input constructors in this crate that take collections use borrowed slices (e.g. ChatPatch.add_members: Option<&[AddMemberInput]>, ChatCreateInput::Group.member_ids: &[&str]). The ChatStreamEnable constructor forces callers to allocate owned Vecs even when they have a slice or a literal. Fix: change the constructor signature to new(data_types: Vec<ChatStreamDataType>, chat_ids: Option<&[Id]>, contact_ids: Option<&[Id]>) and store as Option<Vec<Id>> internally by calling .map(|s| s.to_vec()). Alternatively keep Vec for the struct fields and change only the constructor.
